claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by learning a bit about your situation.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to gather some more details.

Since Alex is 17 years old, could you tell me: Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school but he's not studying anything right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

First, could you tell me: Are you responsible for Alex? This means does Alex live with you, or do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be worth towards his costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

Do you live in the UK, and are you either a UK citizen or have the right to reside in the UK (for example, through settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just to confirm a few final details:

1. Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex based on the information you've provided.

Before I make my final determination, let me review the official eligibility criteria to ensure I give you an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now provide you with a determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

While you meet several of the key requirements (you're responsible for Alex as he lives with you, you're a UK citizen living in the UK, and nobody else is claiming for him), there is one critical requirement that is not met:

**For children aged 16 or over**, Child Benefit only continues if the child is **under 20 AND in approved education or training**. Since Alex is 17 and is not currently in any education or training, you don't qualify for Child Benefit for him.

**However**, if Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future (such as college, sixth form, or certain training programs), you may become eligible again. In that case, you would be able to make a new claim for Child Benefit.

If you have any questions about what counts as approved education or training, or if Alex's circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for guidance.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, while you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, if your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify ONLY if they are under 20 AND stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in any education or training, you do not meet the criteria for Child Benefit for him. The fact that Alex lives with you and you are responsible for him, you live in the UK as a UK citizen, and nobody else is claiming for him are all met, but the critical requirement about education/training for a 17-year-old is not satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 17-year-old son Alex living at home. While the claimant is responsible for Alex and meets residency requirements, Alex does not meet the age-related education requirement. For children aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 and in approved education or training to qualify. Since Alex is 17 and not in any education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is not in any education or training, you do not meet the criteria for Child Benefit for him. The fact that Alex lives with you and you are responsible for him, you live in the UK as a UK citizen, and nobody else is claiming for him are all met, but the critical requirement about education/training for a 17-year-old is not satisfied.

While the claimant is responsible for Alex and meets residency requirements, Alex does not meet the age-related education requirement. For children aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 and in approved education or training to qualify. Since Alex is 17 and not in any education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
